title: Psalm L

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much availeth the Word of God to us for the correction of our life, both regarding His rewards to be expected, and His punishments to be feared, let each one measure in himself; and let him put his conscience without deceit before His eyes, and not flatter himself in a danger so great: for ye see that even our Lord God Himself doth flatter no one: though He comforteth us by promising His blessings, and by strengthening our hope; yet them that live ill and despise His word He assuredly spareth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let each one examine himself, while it is time, and let him see where he is, and either persevere in good, or be changed from evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as he saith in this Psalm, not any man whatever nor any angel whatever, but, “The Lord, the God of gods, hath spoken” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in speaking, He hath done what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath called the earth from the rising of the sun unto the going down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that “hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down,” is Our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, “the Word made Flesh,” in order that He might dwell in us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ then is the “God of gods;” because by Himself were all things made, and without Himself was nothing made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Word of God, if He is God, is truly the God of gods; but whether He be God the Gospel answereth, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if all things were made by Himself, as He saith in the sequel, then if any were made gods, by Himself were they made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the one God was not made, and He is Himself alone truly God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Himself the only God, Father and Son and Holy Ghost, is one God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But then who are those gods, or where are they, of whom God is the true God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another Psalm saith, “God hath stood in the synagogue of gods, but in the midst He judgeth gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As yet we know not whether perchance any gods be congregated in heaven, and in their congregation, for this is “in the synagogue,” God hath stood to judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See in the same Psalm those to whom he saith, “I have said, Ye are gods, and children of the Highest all; but ye shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is evident then, that He hath called men gods, that are deified of His Grace, not born of His Substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He doth justify, who is just through His own self, and not of another; and He doth deify who is God through Himself, not by the partaking of another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He that justifieth doth Himself deify, in that by justifying He doth make sons of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He hath given them power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we have been made sons of God, we have also been made gods: but this is the effect of Grace adopting, not of nature generating.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the only Son of God, God, and one God with the Father, Our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, was in the beginning the Word, and the Word with God, the Word God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rest that are made gods, are made by His own Grace, are not born of His Substance, that they should be the same as He, but that by favour they should come to Him, and be fellow-heirs with Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so great is the love in Him the Heir, that He hath willed to have fellow-heirs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What covetous man would will this, to have fellow-heirs?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even one that is found so to will, will share with them the inheritance, the sharer having less himself, than if he had possessed alone: but the inheritance wherein we are fellow-heirs of Christ, is not lessened by multitude of possessors, nor is it made narrower by the number of fellow-heirs: but is as great for many as it is for few, as great for individuals as for all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “See,” saith the Apostle, “what love God hath bestowed upon us, that we should be called, and be, the sons of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in another place, “Dearly beloved, we are the sons of God, and it doth not yet appear what we shall be.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are therefore in hope, not yet in substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But we know,” he saith, “that when He shall have appeared, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Only Son is like Him by birth, we like by seeing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are not like in such sort as He, who is the same as He is by whom He was begotten: for we are like, not equal: He, because equal, is therefore like.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have heard who are the gods that being made are justified, because they are called the sons of God: and who are the gods that are not Gods, to whom the God of gods is terrible?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For another Psalm saith, “He is terrible over all gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if thou shouldest enquire, what gods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith, “For all the gods of the nations are devils.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the gods of the nations, to the devils, terrible: to the gods made by Himself, to sons, lovely.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Furthermore, I find both of them confessing the Majesty of God, both the devils confessed Christ, and the faithful confessed Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art Christ, the Son of the living God,” said Peter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We know who Thou art, Thou art the Son of God,” said the devils.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A like confession I hear, but like love I find not; nay even here love, there fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom therefore He is lovely, the same are sons; to whom He is terrible, are not sons; to whom He is lovely, the same He hath made gods; those to whom He is terrible He doth prove not to be gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these are made gods, those are reputed gods; these Truth maketh gods, those error doth so account.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The God,” therefore, “of gods, the Lord hath spoken” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath spoken many ways.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By Angels He hath Himself spoken, by Prophets He hath Himself spoken, by His own mouth He hath Himself spoken, by His faithful He doth Himself speak, by our lowliness, when we say anything true, He doth Himself speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See then, by speaking diversely, many ways, by many vessels, by many instruments, yet He doth Himself sound everywhere, by touching, moulding, inspiring: see what He hath done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “He hath spoken, and hath called the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Africa, perhaps! for the sake of those that say, the Church of Christ is the portion of Donatus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Africa indeed alone He hath not called, but even Africa He hath not severed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He that “hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down,” leaving out no parts that He hath not called, in His calling hath found Africa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it rejoice therefore in unity, not pride itself in division.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We say well, that the voice of the God of gods hath come even into Africa, hath not stayed in Africa.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “He hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no place where may lurk the conspiracies of heretics, they have no place wherein they may hide themselves under the shadow of falsehood; for “there is none that can hide himself from the heat thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that hath called the world, hath called even the whole world: He that hath called the world, hath called as much as He hath formed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do false christs and false prophets rise up against me? why is it that they strive to ensnare me with captious words, saying, “Lo! here is Christ, Lo!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is there!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I hear not them that point out portions: the God of gods hath pointed out the whole: “He” that “hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down,” hath redeemed the whole; but hath condemned them that lay false claim to portions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we have heard the world called from the rising of the sun unto the going down: whence doth He begin to call, who hath called?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thing also hear ye: “Out of Sion is the semblance of His beauty” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evidently the Psalm doth agree with the Gospel, which saith, “Throughout all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, “Throughout all nations:” He hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, “Beginning at Jerusalem:” “Out of Sion is the semblance of His beauty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “He hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down,” agreeth with the words of the Lord, who saith, “It behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day; and that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in His Name throughout all nations.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all nations are from the rising of the sun unto the going down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that, “Out of Sion is the semblance of His beauty,” that thence beginneth the beauty of His Gospel, that thence He began to be preached, being “beautiful in form beyond the sons of men,” agreeth with the words of the Lord, who saith, “Beginning at Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: New things are in tune with old, old things with new: the two Seraphim say to one another, “Holy, holy, holy, Lord God of Sabaoth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The two Testaments are both in tune, and the two Testaments have one voice: let the voice of the Testaments in tune be heard, not that of pretenders disinherited.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thing then hath the God of gods done, “He hath called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down, His semblance going before out of Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that place were His disciples, who received the Holy Ghost sent from heaven on the fiftieth day after His resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence the Gospel, thence the preaching, thence the whole world filled, and that in the Grace of Faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when the Lord Himself had come, because He came to suffer, He came hidden: and though He was strong in Himself, He appeared in the flesh weak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He must needs appear in order that He might not be perceived; be despised, in order that He might be slain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was semblance of glory in divinity, but it lay concealed in flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if they had known, they would never have crucified the Lord of glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then He walked hidden among the Jews, among His enemies, doing marvels, suffering ills, until He was hanged on the tree, and the Jews seeing Him hanging both despised Him the more, and before the Cross wagging their heads they said, “If He be the Son of God, let Him come down from the Cross.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hidden then was the God of gods, and He gave forth words more out of compassion for us than out of His own majesty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whence, unless assumed from us, were those words, “My God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when hath the Father forsaken the Son, or the Son the Father?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not Father and Son one God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then, “My God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me,” save that in the Flesh of infirmity there was acknowledged the voice of a sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as He took upon Him the likeness of the flesh of sin, why should He not take upon Him the voice of sin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hidden then was the God of gods, both when He walked among men, and when He hungered, and when He thirsted, and when fatigued He sat, and when with wearied body He slept, and when taken, and when scourged, and when standing before the judge, and when He made answer to him in his pride, “Thou couldest have no power against Me, except it had been given thee from above;” and while led as a victim “before His shearer He opened not His mouth,” and while crucified, and while buried, He was always hidden God of gods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What took place after He rose again?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The disciples marvelled, and at first believed not, until they touched and handled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But flesh had risen, because flesh had been dead: Divinity which could not die, even still lay hid in the flesh of Him rising.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Form could be seen, limbs held, scars handled: the Word by whom all things were made, who doth see? who doth hold? who doth handle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet “the Word was made flesh, and dwelled among us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And Thomas, that was holding Man, understood God as he was able.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he had handled the scars, he cried out, “My Lord, and my God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet the Lord was showing that form, and that flesh, which they had seen upon the Cross, which had been laid in the sepulchre.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He stayed with them forty days.…But what was said to Thomas handling?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because thou hast seen, thou hast believed; blessed are they that see not, and believe.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are foretold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That world called from the rising of the sun unto the going down seeth not, and believeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hidden then is the God of gods, both to those among whom He walked, and to those by whom He was crucified, and to those before whose eyes He rose, and to us who believe on Him in heaven sitting, whom we have not seen on earth walking.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even if we were to see, should we not see that which the Jews saw and crucified?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is more, that not seeing we believe Christ to be God, than that they seeing deemed Him only to be man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They in a word by thinking evil slew, we by believing well are made alive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This God of gods, both then hidden, and now hidden, shall He ever be hidden?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evidently not: hear what followeth: “God shall come manifest” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that came hidden, shall come manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hidden He came to be judged, manifest He shall come to judge: hidden He came that He might stand before a judge, manifest He shall come that He may be judge even of judges: “He shall come manifest, and shall not be silent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is He now silent?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence are all the words that we say? whence those precepts? whence those warnings? whence that trumpet of terror?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is not silent, and is silent: is not silent from warning, is silent from avenging: is not silent from precept, is silent from judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He suffereth sinners daily doing evil things, not caring for God, not in their conscience, not in heaven, not in earth: all these things escape Him not, and universally He doth admonish all; and whenever He chastiseth any on earth, it is admonition, not yet condemnation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is silent then from judgment, He is hidden in heaven, as yet He intercedeth for us: He is long-suffering to sinners, not putting forth His wrath, but awaiting penitence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith in another place: “I have held my peace, shall I always hold my peace?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then He shall not hold His peace, “God shall come manifest.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the God Himself, who is our God: for he is not God, who is not our God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the gods of the nations are devils: the God of Christians is very God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Himself shall come, but “manifest,” not still to be mocked, not still to be buffeted and scourged: He shall come, but “manifest,” not still to be smitten with a reed upon the head, not still to be crucified, slain, buried: for all these things God being hidden hath willed to suffer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall come manifest, and shall not be silent.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But that He shall come to judgment, the following words teach.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fire shall go before Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be we changed, and we shall not fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let chaff fear the fire: what doth it to gold?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What thou mayest do is now in thy power, so thou mayest not experience, for want of being corrected, that which is to come even against thy will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if we might so bring it about, brethren, that the day of judgment should not come; I think that even then it were not for us to live ill.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the fire of the day of judgment were not to come, and over sinners there impended only separation from the face of God, in whatever affluence of delights they might be, not seeing Him by whom they were created, and separated from that sweetness of His ineffable countenance, in whatever eternity and impunity of sin, they ought to bemoan themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what shall I say, or to whom shall I say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is a punishment to lovers, not to despisers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They that have begun to feel in any degree the sweetness of wisdom and truth, know what I say, how great a punishment it is to be only separated from the face of God: but they that have not tasted that sweetness, if not yet they yearn for the face of God, let them fear even fire; let punishments terrify those, whom rewards win not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of no value to thee is what God promiseth, tremble at what He threateneth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sweetness of His presence shall come; thou art not changed, thou art not awakened, thou sighest not, thou longest not: thou embracest thy sins and the delights of thy flesh, thou art heaping stubble to thyself, the fire will come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fire shall burn in His presence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This fire will not be like thy hearth-fire, into which nevertheless, if thou art compelled to thrust thy hand, thou wilt do whatsoever he would have thee who doth threaten this alternative.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he say to thee, “write against the life of thy father, write against the lives of thy children, for if thou do not, I thrust thy hand into thy fire:” thou wilt do it in order that thy hand be not burned, in order that thy member be not burned for a time, though it is not to be ever in pain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thine enemy threateneth then but so light an evil, and thou doest evil; God threateneth eternal evil, and doest thou not good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To do evil not even menaces should compel thee: from doing good not even menaces should deter thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by the menaces of God, by menaces of everlasting fire, thou art dissuaded from evil, invited to good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore doth it grieve thee, except because thou believest not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let each one then examine his heart, and see what faith doth hold there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we believe a judgment to come, brethren, let us live well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now is time of mercy, then will be time of judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one will say, “Call me back to my former years.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even then men will repent, but will repent in vain: now let there be repentance, while there is fruit of repentance; now let there be applied to the roots of the tree a basket of dung, sorrow of heart, and tears; lest He come and pluck up by the roots.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when He shall have plucked up, then the fire is to be looked for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, even if the branches have been broken, they can again be grafted in: then, “every tree which bringeth not forth good fruit, shall be cut down, and shall be cast into the fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fire shall burn in His presence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And a mighty tempest round about Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A mighty tempest,” in order to winnow so great a floor.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this tempest shall be that winnowing whereby from the saints shall be put away everything impure, from the faithful every unreality; from godly men and them that fear the Word of God, every scorner and every proud man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now a sort of mixture doth lie there, from the rising of the sun unto the going down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see then how He will do that is to come, what He will do with that tempest which “shall be a mighty tempest round about Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doubtless this tempest is to make a sort of separation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is that separation which they waited not for, who brake the nets, before they came to land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in this separation there is made a sort of distinction between good men and bad men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There be some that now follow Christ with lightened shoulders without the load of the world’s cares, who have not heard in vain, “If thou wilt be perfect, go and sell all that thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow Me;” to which sort is said, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some then shall be judging with the Lord: but others to be judged, but to be placed on the right hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that there will be certain judging with the Lord, we have most evident testimony, which I have but now quoted: “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what the Lord did after His resurrection, signified what is to be to us after our resurrection, in that number of the kingdom of heaven, where shall be no bad man.…Lastly, those seven thousand of whom reply was made to Elias, “I have left me seven thousand men that have not bowed knees before Baal,” far exceed that number of fishes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the hundred and fifty-three fishes doth not alone express just such a number of saints, but Scripture doth express the whole number of saints and righteous men by so great a number for a particular reason; to wit, in order that in those hundred and fifty-three all may be understood that pertain to the resurrection to eternal life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Law hath ten commandments: but the Spirit of Grace, through which alone the Law is fulfilled, is called sevenfold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The number then must be examined, what mean ten and seven: ten in commandments, seven in the grace of the Holy Spirit: by which grace the commandments are fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ten then and seven contain all that pertain to the resurrection, to the right hand, to the kingdom of heaven, to life eternal, that is, they that fulfil the Law by the Grace of the Spirit, not as it were by their own work or their own merit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But ten and seven, if thou countest from one unto seventeen, by adding all the numbers by steps, so that to one thou mayest add two, add three, add four, that they may become ten, by adding five that they may become fifteen, by adding six that they may become twenty-one, by adding seven that they may become twenty-eight, by adding eight that they may become thirty-six, by adding nine that they may become forty-five, by adding ten that they may become fifty-five, by adding eleven that they may become sixty-six, by adding twelve that they may become seventy-eight, by adding thirteen that they may become ninety-one, by adding fourteen that they may become one hundred and five, by adding fifteen that they may become one hundred and twenty, by adding sixteen that they may become one hundred and thirty-six, by adding seventeen, make up one hundred and fifty-three, thou wilt find a vast number of all saints to belong to this number of a few fishes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In like manner then as in five virgins, countless virgins; as in five brethren of him that was tormented in hell, thousands of the people of the Jews; as in the number of one hundred and fifty-three fishes, thousands of thousands of saints: so in twelve thrones, not twelve men, but great is the number of the perfect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I see what is next required of us; in like manner as in the case of the five virgins, a reason was given why many should belong to five, and why to those five many Jews, and why to a hundred and fifty-three many perfect—to show why and how to the twelve thrones not twelve men, but many belong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What mean the twelve thrones, which signify all men everywhere that have been enabled to be so perfect as they must be perfect, to whom it is said, “Ye shall sit over the twelve tribes of Israel”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why do all men everywhere belong to the number twelve?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because the very “everywhere” which we say, we say of the whole world: but the compass of lands is contained in four particular quarters, East, West, South, and North: from all these quarters they being called in the Trinity and made perfect in the faith and precept of the Trinity,—seeing that three times four are twelve, ye perceive wherefore the saints belong to the whole world; they that shall sit upon twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel, since the twelve tribes of Israel, also, are the twelve tribes of the whole of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For like as they that are to judge are from the whole world, so also they that are to be judged are from the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle Paul of himself, when he was reproving believing laymen, because they referred not their causes to the Church, but dragged them with whom they had matters before the public, said, “Know ye not that we shall judge Angels?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See after what sort He hath made Himself judge: not only himself, but also all that judge aright in the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since then it is evident, that many are to judge with the Lord, but that others are to be judged, not however on equality, but according to their deserts; He will come with all His Angels, when before Him shall be gathered all nations, and among all the Angels are to be reckoned those that have been made so perfect, that sitting upon twelve thrones they judge the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For men are called Angels: the Apostle saith of himself, “As an angel of God ye received me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of John Baptist it is said, “Behold, I send My Angel before Thy face, that shall prepare Thy way before Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, coming with all Angels, together with Him He shall have the Saints also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For plainly saith Isaias also, “He shall come to judgment with the elders of the people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those “elders of the people,” then, those but now named Angels, those thousands of many men made perfect coming from the whole world, are called Heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the others are called earth, yet fruitful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which is the earth that is fruitful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is to be set on the right hand, unto which it shall be said, “I was an hungred, and ye gave Me to eat:” truly fruitful earth in which the Apostle doth joy, when they sent to him to supply his necessities: “Not because I ask a gift,” he saith, “but I require fruit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he giveth thanks, saying, “Because at length ye have budded forth again to be thoughtful for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith, “Ye have budded forth again,” as to trees which had withered away with a kind of barrenness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the Lord coming to judgment (that we may now hear the Psalm, brethren), He will do what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He will call the heaven from above” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The heaven, all the Saints, those made perfect that shall judge, them He shall call from above, to be sitters with Him to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how shall “He call the heaven from above,” when the heaven is always above?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But those that He here calleth heaven, the same elsewhere He calleth heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What heavens?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That tell out the glory of God: for, “The heavens tell out the glory of God:” whereof is said, “Into all the earth their sound hath gone forth, and into the ends of the world their words.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see the Lord severing in judgment: “He shall call the heaven from above and the earth, to sever His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whom but from evil men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of whom here afterwards no mention is made, now as it were condemned to punishment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See these good men, and distinguish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall call the heaven from above, and the earth, to sever His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He calleth the earth also, not however to be associated, but to be dissociated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at first He called them together, “when the God of gods spake and called the world from the rising of the sun unto the going down,” He had not yet severed: those servants had been sent to bid to the marriage, who had gathered good and bad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when the God of gods shall come manifest and shall not keep silence, He shall so call the “heaven from above” that it may judge with Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what the heaven is, the heavens themselves are; just as what the earth is, the lands themselves, just as what the Church is, the Churches themselves: “He shall call the heaven from above, and the earth, to sever His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now with the heaven He severeth the earth, that is, the heaven with Him doth sever the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth He sever the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such sort that He setteth on the right hand some, others on the left.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But to the earth severed, He saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive the kingdom which was prepared for you from the beginning of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I was an hungred, and ye gave me to eat,” and so forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they say, “When saw we Thee an hungred?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And He, “Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of Mine, ye have done it unto Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall call therefore the heaven from above, and the earth, to sever His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Gather to Him His righteous” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The voice divine and prophetic, seeing future things as if present doth exhort the Angels gathering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He shall send His Angels, and before Him shall be gathered all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Gather to Him His righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What righteous men save those that live of faith and do works of mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those works are works of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast the Gospel: “Beware of doing your righteousness before men to be seen of them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as if it were inquired, What righteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When therefore thou doest alms,” He saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore alms He hath signified to be works of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those very persons gather for His righteous: gather those that have had compassion on the “needy,” that have considered the needy and poor: gather them, “The Lord preserve them, and make them to live;” “Gather to Him His righteous: who order His covenant above sacrifices:” that is, who think of His promises above those things which they work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For those things are sacrifices, God saying, “I will have mercy more than sacrifice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who keep His covenant more than sacrifice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the Heaven shall declare His righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly this righteousness of God to us the “heavens have declared,” the Evangelists have foretold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through them we have heard that some will be on the right hand, to whom the Householder saith, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, receive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Receive what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A kingdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In return for what thing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was an hungred, and ye gave Me to eat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What so valueless, what so earthly, as to break bread to the hungry?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At so much is valued the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Break thy bread to the hungry, and the needy without covering bring into thy house; if thou seest one naked, clothe him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hast not the means of breaking bread, hast not house into which thou mayest bring, hast not garment wherewith thou mayest cover: give a cup of cold water, cast two mites into the treasury.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As much the widow doth buy with two mites, as Peter buyeth, by leaving the nets, as Zacchæus buyeth by giving half his goods.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of so much worth is all that thou hast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The heavens shall declare His righteousness, for God is Judge.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly judge not confounding but severing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “the Lord knoweth them that are His.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even if grains lie hid in the chaff, they are known to the husbandman.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one fear that he is a grain even among the chaff; the eyes of our winnower are not deceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not lest that tempest, which shall be round about Him, should confound thee with chaff.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly mighty will be the tempest; yet not one grain will it sweep from the side of the corn to the chaff: because not any rustic with three-pronged fork, but God, Three in One, is Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the heavens shall declare His righteousness: for God is Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let heavens go, let the heavens tell, into every land let their sound go out, and unto the ends of the world their words: and let that body say, “From the ends of the world unto Thee have I cried, when my heart was in heaviness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now mingled it groaneth, divided it shall rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it cry then and say, “Destroy not my soul with ungodly men, and with men of blood my life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He destroyeth not together, because God is Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it cry to Him and say, “Judge me, O Lord, and sever my cause from the nation unholy:” let it say, He shall do it: there shall be gathered to Him His righteous ones.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath called the earth that He may sever His people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear, my people, and I will speak to thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He shall come and shall not keep silence; see how that even now, if ye hear, He is not silent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear, my people, and I will speak to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou hearest not, I will not speak to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear, and I will speak to thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou hearest not, even though I shall speak, it will not be to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then shall I speak to thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hearest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When hearest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou art my people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Hear, my people:” thou hearest not if thou art an alien people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear, my people, and I will speak to thee: Israel, and I will testify to thee.”…For “Thy God,” is properly said to that man whom God doth keep more as one of His family, as though in His household, as though in His peculiar: “Thy God am I.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wilt thou more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Requirest thou a reward from God, so that God may give thee something; so that what He hath given thee may be thine own?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold God Himself, who shall give, is thine own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What richer than He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Gifts thou wast desiring, thou hast the Giver Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God, thy God, I am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What He requireth of man, let us see; what tribute our God, our Emperor and our King doth enjoin us; since He hath willed to be our King, and hath willed us to be His province?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear His injunctions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not a poor man tremble beneath the injunction of God: what God enjoineth to be given to Himself, He doth Himself first give that enjoineth: be ye only devoted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God doth not exact what He hath not given, and to all men hath given what He doth exact.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what doth He exact?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear now: “I will not reprove thee because of thy sacrifices” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will not say to thee, Wherefore hast thou not slain for me a fat bull? why hast thou not selected the best he-goat from thy flock?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore doth that ram amble among thy sheep, and is not laid upon mine altar?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will not say, Examine thy fields and thy pen and thy walls, seeking what thou mayest give Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not reprove thee because of thy sacrifices.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then: Dost Thou not accept my sacrifices?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thy holocausts are always in My sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certain holocausts concerning which it is said in another Psalm, “If Thou hadst desired sacrifice, I would surely have given, with holocausts Thou wilt not be delighted:” and again he turneth himself, “Sacrifice to God is a troubled spirit, a heart broken and humbled God doth not despise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which be then holocausts that He despiseth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which holocausts that are always in His sight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Kindly, O Lord,” he saith, “deal in Thy good will with Sion, and be the walls of Jerusalem builded, then shalt Thou accept the sacrifice of righteousness, oblations, and holocausts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith that certain holocausts God will accept.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is a holocaust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A whole consumed with fire: causis is burning, holon is whole: but a “holocaust” is a whole consumed with fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a certain fire of most burning love: be the mind inflamed with love, let the same love hurry off the limbs to its use, let it not allow them to serve cupidity, in order that we may wholly glow with fire of divine love that will offer to God a holocaust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such “holocausts of thine are in My sight always.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As yet that Israel perchance doth not understand what are the holocausts thereof which He hath in His sight always, and is still thinking of oxen, of sheep, of he-goats: let it not so think: “I will not accept calves of thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Holocausts I named; at once in mind and thought to earthly flocks thou wast running, therefrom thou wast selecting for Me some fat thing: “I will not accept calves of thy house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is foretelling the New Testament, wherein all those sacrifices have ceased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were then foretelling a certain Sacrifice which was to be, with the Blood whereof we should be cleansed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will not accept calves of thy house, nor he-goats of thy flocks.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For mine are all the beasts of the wood” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why should I ask of thee what I have made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it more thine, to whom I have given it to possess, than Mine, who have made it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For mine are all the beasts of the wood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perchance that Israel saith, The beasts are God’s, those wild beasts which I enclose not in my pen, which I bind not to my stall; but this ox and sheep and he-goat—these are mine own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Cattle on the mountain, and oxen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Mine are those which thou possessest not, Mine are these which thou possessest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou art My servant, the whole of thy property is Mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it cannot be, that is the property of the master which the servant hath gotten to himself, and yet that not be the property of the Master which the Master Himself hath created for the servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore Mine are the beasts of the wood which thou hast not taken; Mine are also the cattle on the mountains which are thine, and the oxen which are at thy stall: all are Mine own, for I have created them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I know all the winged creatures of heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth He know?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath weighed them, hath counted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which of us knoweth all the winged creatures of heaven?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even though to some man God give knowledge of all the winged creatures of heaven, He doth not Himself know in the same manner as He giveth man to know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One thing is God’s knowledge, another man’s: in like manner as there is one possession of God’s, another of man’s: that is, God’s possessing is one thing, man’s another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what thou possessest thou hast not wholly in thy power, or else thy ox, so long as it liveth, is in thy power; so as that it either die not, or be not to be fed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With whom there is the highest power, there is highest and most secret cognition.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us ascribe this to God, while praising God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not dare to say, How knoweth God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not, I pray you, brethren, of me expect this, that I should unfold to you, how God doth know: this only I say, He doth not so know as a man, He doth not so know as an Angel: and how He knoweth I dare not say, because also I cannot ken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One thing, nevertheless, I ken, that even before all the winged creatures of heaven were, God knew that which He was to create.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that knowledge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O man, thou beginnest to see, after that thou hadst been formed, after that thou hadst received sense of seeing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These fowls sprung of the water at the word of God, saying, “Let the waters bring forth fowls.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereby did God know the things which He commanded the water to bear forth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now surely He knew what He had created, and before He created He knew.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So great then is the knowledge of God, so that with Himself they were in a certain ineffable manner before they were created: and of thee doth He expect to receive what He had, before He created?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I know all the winged creatures of heaven,” which thou to Me canst not give.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The things which thou wast about to slay for Me, I know all: not because I made I know, but in order that I might make.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the beauty of the field is with Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The fairness of the field, the abundance of all things engendering upon earth, “is with Me,” He saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How with Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were they so, even before they were made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, for with Him were all things to come, and with Him are all things by-gone: things to come in such sort, that there be not withdrawn from Him all things by-gone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With Him are all things by a certain cognition of the ineffable wisdom of God residing in the Word, and the Word Himself is all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is not the beauty of the field in a manner with Him, inasmuch as He is everywhere, and Himself hath said, “Heaven and earth I fill”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What with Him is not, of whom it is said, “If I shall have ascended into heaven, Thou art there; and if I shall have descended into hell, Thou art present”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With Him is the whole: but it is not so with Him as that He doth suffer any contamination from those things which He hath created, or any want of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For with thee, perchance, is a pillar near which thou art standing, and when thou art weary, thou leanest against it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou needest that which is with thee, God needeth not the field which is which Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With Him is field, with Him beauty of earth, with Him beauty of heaven, with Him all winged creatures, because He is Himself everywhere.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wherefore are all things near Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because even before that all things were, or were created, to Him were known all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can explain, who expound that which is said to Him in another Psalm, “For my goods Thou needest not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath said that He needeth not from us any necessary thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I shall be hungry, I will not tell thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that keepeth Israel shall neither hunger nor thirst, nor be weary, nor fall asleep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, lo! according to thy carnality I speak: because thou wilt suffer hunger when thou hast not eaten, perhaps thou thinkest even God doth hunger that He may eat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even though He shall be hungry, He telleth not thee: all things are before Him, whence He will He taketh what is needful for Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These words are said to convince little understanding; not that God hath declared His hunger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Though for our sake this God of gods deigned even to hunger.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He came to hunger, and to fill; He came to thirst, and give drink; He came to be clothed with mortality, and to clothe with immortality; He came poor, to make rich.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He lost not His riches by taking to Him our poverty, for, “In him are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I shall be hungry, I will not tell thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Mine is the whole world, and the fulness thereof.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not then labour to find what to give Me, without whom I have what I will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then dost still think of thy flocks?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall I eat the flesh of bulls, or shall I drink the blood of he-goats?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard what of us He requireth not, who willeth to enjoin us somewhat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If of such things ye were thinking, now withdraw your thoughts from such things: think not to offer God any such thing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou hast a fat bull, kill for the poor: let them eat the flesh of bulls, though they shall not drink the blood of he-goats.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which, when thou shalt have done, He will account it to thee, that hath said, “If I shall be hungry, I will not tell thee:” and He shall say to thee, “I was hungry, and thou gavest Me to eat.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall I eat the flesh of bulls, or shall I drink the blood of he-goats?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say then, Lord our God, what dost Thou enjoin thy people, Thy Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us also say to Him, “In me, O God, are thy vows, which I will render of prose to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I had feared lest Thou mightest enjoin something which would be out of my power, which I was counting to be in my pen, and but now perchance it had been taken away by a thief.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost Thou enjoin me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me revert to myself, wherein I may find what I may immolate: let me revert to myself; in myself may I find immolation of praise: be Thy altar my conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are without anxiety, we go not into Arabia in quest of frankincense: not any bags of covetous dealer do we sift: God requireth of us the sacrifice of praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Zacchæus had the sacrifice of praise in his patrimony; the widow had it in her bag; some poor host or other hath had it in his jar: another neither in patrimony, nor in bag, nor in jar, hath had anything, had it wholly in his heart: salvation was to the house of Zacchæus; and more this poor widow cast in than those rich men: this man, that doth offer a cup of cold water, shall not lose his reward: but there is even “peace on earth to men of good will.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O sacrifice gratuitous, by grace given!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have not indeed bought this to offer, but Thou hast given: for not even this should I have had.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is the immolation of the sacrifice of praise, to render thanks to Him from whom thou hast whatever of good thou hast, and by whose mercy is forgiven thee whatsoever of evil of thine thou hast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise: and render to the Highest thy prayers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With this odour the Lord is well pleased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And call thou upon Me in the day of thy tribulation: and I will draw thee forth, and thou shalt glorify Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou oughtest not to rely on thy powers, all thy aids are deceitful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon Me call thou in the day of tribulation: I will draw thee forth, and thou shalt glorify Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to this end I have allowed the day of tribulation to come to thee: because perchance if thou wast not troubled, thou wouldest not call on Me: but when thou art troubled, thou callest on Me; when thou callest upon Me, I will draw thee forth; when I shall draw thee forth, thou shalt glorify Me, that thou mayest no more depart from Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A certain man had grown dull and cold in fervour of prayer, and said, “Tribulation and grief I found, and on the Name of the Lord I called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He found tribulation as it were some profitable thing; he had rotted in the slough of his sins; now he had continued without feeling, he found tribulation to be a sort of caustic and cutting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I found,” he saith, “tribulation and grief, and on the Name of the Lord I called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And truly, brethren, tribulations are known to all men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold those afflictions that abound in mankind; one afflicted with loss bewaileth; another smitten with bereavement mourneth; another exiled from country grieveth and desireth to return, deeming sojourning intolerable; another’s vineyard is hailed upon, he observeth his labours and all his toil spent in vain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When can a human being not be made sad?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An enemy he findeth in a friend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What greater misery in mankind?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things all men do deplore and grieve at, and these are tribulations: in all these they call upon the Lord, and they do rightly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them call upon God, He is able either to teach how it must be borne, or to heal it when borne.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knoweth how not to suffer us to be tried above that we are able to bear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us call upon God even in those tribulations: but these tribulations do find us; as in another Psalm is written, “Helper in tribulations which have found us too much:” there is a certain tribulation which we ought to find.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let such tribulations find us: there is a certain tribulation which we ought to seek and to find.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The above-named felicity in this world, abundance of temporal things: that is not indeed tribulation, these are the solaces of our tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what tribulation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of our sojourning.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the very fact that we are not yet with God, the very fact that we are living amid trials and difficulties, that we cannot be without fear, is tribulation: for there is not that peace which is promised us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that shall not have found this tribulation in his sojourning, doth not think of going home to his father-land.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is tribulation, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely now we do good works, when we deal bread to the hungry, home to the stranger, and the like: tribulation even this is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we find pitiful objects upon whom we show pity; and the pitiful case of pitiful objects maketh us compassionate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How much better now would it be with thee in that place, where thou findest no hungry man whom thou mayest feed, where thou findest no stranger whom thou mayest take in, no naked man whom thou mayest cover, no sick man whom thou mayest visit, no litigant whom thou mayest set at one!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all things in that place are most high, are true, are holy, are everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our bread in that place is righteousness, our drink there is wisdom, our garment there is immortality, our house is everlasting in the heavens, our stedfastness is immortality: doth sickness come over?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth weariness weigh down to sleep?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No death, no litigation: there peace, quiet, joy, righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No enemy hath entrance, no friend falleth away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the quiet there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we think and observe where we are, and where He that cannot lie hath promised that we are to be, from His very promise we find in what tribulation we are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This tribulation none findeth, but he that shall have sought it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art whole, see if thou art miserable; for it is easy for him that is sick to find himself miserable: when thou art whole, see if thou art miserable; that thou art not yet with God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Tribulation and grief I found, and on the Name of the Lord I called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Immolate,” therefore, “to God the sacrifice of praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise Him promising, praise Him calling, praise Him exhorting, praise Him helping: and understand in what tribulation thou art placed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Call upon (Him), thou shalt be drawn forth, thou shalt glorify, shalt abide.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But see what followeth, my brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now some one or other, because God had said to him, “Immolate to God the sacrifice of praise,” and had enjoined in a manner this tribute, did meditate to himself and said, I will rise daily, I will proceed to Church, I will say one hymn at matins, another at vespers, a third or fourth in my house, daily I do sacrifice the sacrifice of praise, and immolate to my God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well thou doest indeed, if thou doest this: but take heed, lest now thou be careless, because now thou doest this: and perchance thy tongue bless God, and thy life curse God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O my people, saith to thee the God of gods, the Lord that spake, “calling the earth from the rising of the sun unto the setting,” though yet thou art placed amid the tares, “Immolate the sacrifice of praise to thy God, and render to Him thy prayers:” but take heed lest thou live ill, and chant well.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Unto the sinner, saith God, why dost thou tell out My judgments, and takest My Covenant in thy mouth?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see, brethren, with what trembling we say these words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We take the Covenant of God in our mouth, and we say these words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We take the Covenant of God in our mouth, and we preach to you the instruction and judgment of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what saith God to the sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Why dost thou?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth He then forbid preachers that be sinners?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where is that, “What they say do, but what they do, do not”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is that, “Whether in truth or on occasion Christ be preached”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But these words were said, lest they should fear that hear, from whomsoever it be that they hear: not that they should be without care that speak good words, and do evil deeds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now therefore, brethren, ye are without care: if ye hear good words ye hear God, through whomsoever it be that ye may hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God would not dismiss without reproof them that speak: lest with their speaking alone, without care for themselves they should slumber in evil life, and say to themselves, “For God will not consign us to perdition, through whose mouth He has willed that so many good words should be spoken to His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but hear what thou speakest, whoever thou art that speakest: and thou that writ be heard thyself, first hear thyself; and speak what a certain man doth speak in another Psalm, “I will hear what in me speaketh the Lord God, for He shall speak peace to His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What am I then, that hear not what in me He speaketh, and will that other hear what through me He speaketh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will hear first, will hear, and chiefly I will hear what speaketh in me the Lord God, for He shall speak peace to His people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me hear, and “chasten my body, and to servitude subject it, lest perchance to others preaching, myself be found a cast-away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Why dost thou tell out my judgments?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore to thee what profiteth not thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He admonisheth him to hear: not to lay down preaching, but to take up obedience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thou, why dost thou take My Covenant in they mouth?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thou hatest instruction” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hatest discipline.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When I spare, thou singest and praisest: when I chasten, thou murmurest: as though, when I spare, I am thy God: and, when I chasten, I am not thy God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I rebuke and chasten those whom I love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But thou hatest instruction: and hast thrown My sayings behind thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words that are said through thee, thou throwest behind thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And thou hast thrown My sayings behind thee:” to a place where they may not be seen by thee, but may load thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And thou hast thrown My sayings behind thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If thou sawest a thief, thou didst consent unto him, and with adulterers thou didst make thy portion” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest perchance thou shouldest say, I have not committed theft, I have not committed adultery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What if he pleased thee that hath committed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou not with the very pleasing consented?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou not by approval made thy portion with him that hath committed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this is, brethren, to consent with a thief, and to make with an adulterer thy portion: for even if thou committest not, and approvest what is committed, thou art an accessory in the deed: for “the sinner is praised in the longings of his soul, and he that doeth iniquity shall be blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou doest not evil things, thou praisest evil-doers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For is this a small evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou didst make thy portion with adulterers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy mouth hath abounded in malice, and thy tongue hath embraced deceit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the malevolence and deceit, brethren, of certain men he speaketh, who by adulation, though they know what they hear to be evil, yet lest they offend those from whom they hear, not only by not reproving but by holding their peace do consent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Too little is it, that they do not say, Thou hast done evil: but they even say, Thou hast done even well: and they know it to be evil: but their mouth aboundeth in malice, and their tongue embraceth deceit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deceit is a sort of guile in words, of uttering one thing, thinking another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith not, thy tongue hath committed deceit or perpetrated deceit, but in order to point out to thee a kind of pleasure taken in the very evil doing, He hath said, “Hath embraced.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is too little that thou doest it, thou art delighted too; thou praisest openly, thou laughest to thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou dost push to destruction a man heedlessly putting forth his faults, and knowing not whether they be faults: thou that knowest it to be a fault, sayest not, “Whither art thou rushing?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
