verses.text: If thou wert to see him heedlessly walk in the dark, where thou knewest a well to be, and wert to hold thy peace, of what sort wouldest thou be? wouldest thou not be set down for an enemy of his life?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet if he were to fall into a well, not in soul but in body he would die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth fall headlong into his vices, he doth expose before thee his evil doings: thou knowest them to be evil, and praisest and laughest to thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Oh that at length he were to be turned to God at whom thou laughest, and whom thou wouldest not reprove, and that he were to say, “Let them be confounded that say to me, Well, well.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sitting against thy brother thou didst detract” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this “sitting” doth belong to that whereof he hath spoken above in, “hath embraced.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that doeth anything while standing or passing along, doth it not with pleasure: but if he for this purpose sitteth, how much leisure doth he seek out to do it!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That very evil detraction thou wast making with diligence, thou wast making sitting; thou wouldest thereon be wholly engaged; thou wast embracing thy evil, thou wast kissing thy craftiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And against thy mother’s son thou didst lay a stumbling-block.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is “mother’s son”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it not brother?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would repeat then the same that he had said above, “thy brother.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hath he intimated that any distinction must be perceived by us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Evidently, brethren, I think a distinction must be made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brother against brother doth detract, for example’s sake, as though for instance one strong, and now a doctor and scholar of some weight, doth detract from his brother, one perchance that is teaching well and walking well: but another is weak, against him he layeth a stumbling-block by detracting from the former.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when the good are detracted from by those that seem to be of some weight and to be learned, the weak fall upon the stumbling-block, who as yet know not how to judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore this weak one is called “mother’s son,” not yet father’s, still needing milk, and hanging on the breast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is borne as yet in the bosom of his mother the Church, he is not strong enough to draw near to the solid food of his Father’s table, but from the mother’s breast he draweth sustenance, unskilled in judging, inasmuch as yet he is animal and carnal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the spiritual man judgeth all things,” but “the animal man perceiveth not those things which are of the Spirit of God; for they are foolishness to him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To such men saith the Apostle, “I could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, as to babes in Christ I gave you milk to drink, not meat; for ye were not able, but not even now are ye able.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A mother I have been to you: as is said in another place, “I became a babe among you, even as a nurse cherishing her own children.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not a nurse nursing children of others, but a nurse cherishing her own children.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are mothers who when they have borne give to nurses: they that have borne cherish not their children, because they have given them to be nursed; but those that cherish, cherish not their own, but those of others: but he himself had borne, he was himself cherishing, to no nurse did commit what he had borne; for he had said, “Of whom I travail again until Christ be formed in you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He did cherish them, and gave milk.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there were some as it were learned and spiritual men who detracted from Paul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His letters indeed, say they, are weighty and powerful; but the presence of his body weak, and speech contemptible:” he saith himself in his Epistle, that certain his detractors had said these words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were sitting, and were detracting against their brother, and against that their mother’s son, to be fed with milk, they were laying a stumbling-block.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And against thy mother’s son thou didst lay a stumbling-block.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “These things hast thou done, and I held my tongue” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the Lord our God shall come, and shall not keep silence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, “These things hast thou done, and I held my tongue.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “I held my tongue”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From vengeance I have desisted, my severity I have deferred, patience to thee I have prolonged, thy repentance I have long looked for…“Thou hast imagined iniquity, that I shall be like unto thee;” Thou hast imagined that I shall be like unto thee, while thou wilt not be like unto Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Be ye,” he saith, “perfect, even as your Father, which is in the heavens, who maketh His sun to rise on the good and evil.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Him thou wouldest not copy, who giveth good things even to evil men, insomuch that sitting thou dost detract even from good men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will reprove thee,” when “God manifest shall come, our God, and shall not keep silence,” “I will reprove thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what to thee shall I do in reproving thee? what to thee shall I do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now thyself thou seest not, I will make thee see thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because if thou shouldest see thyself, and shouldest displease thyself, thou wouldest please Me: but because not seeing thyself thou hast pleased thyself, thou wilt displease both Me and thyself; Me when thou shalt be judged; thyself when thou shalt burn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what to thee shall I do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will set thee before thy face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For why wouldest thou escape thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At thy back thou art to thyself, thou seest not thyself: I make thee see thyself: what behind thy back thou hast put, before thy face will I put; thou shalt see thy uncleanness, not that thou mayest amend, but that thou mayest blush.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, “understand these things, ye that forget God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how He crieth, and keepeth not silence, spareth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hadst forgotten the Lord, didst not think of thy evil life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perceive how thou hast forgotten the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lest at length He seize like a lion, and there be none to deliver.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “like a lion”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Like a brave one, like a mighty one, like him whom none can withstand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To this he made reference when he said, “Lion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is used for praise, it is used also for showing evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The devil hath been called lion: “Your adversary,” He saith, “like a roaring lion, goeth about seeking whom He may devour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May it not be that whereas he hath been called lion because of savage fierceness, Christ hath been called Lion for wondrous mightiness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where is that, “The Lion hath prevailed of the tribe of Judah?”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sacrifice of praise shall glorify Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall “sacrifice of praise glorify Me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Assuredly sacrifice of praise doth no wise profit evil men, because they take Thy Covenant in their mouth, and do damnable things that displease Thine eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Straightway, he saith, even to them this I say, “Sacrifice of praise shall glorify Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou livest ill and speakest good words, not yet dost thou praise: but again, if, when thou beginnest to live well, to thy merits thou dost ascribe thy living well, not yet dost thou praise.…Therefore the Publican went down justified, rather than that Pharisee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore hear ye that live well, hear ye that live ill: “Sacrifice of praise shall glorify Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one offereth Me this sacrifice, and is evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I say not, Let there not offer Me this any one that is evil; but no one doth offer Me this, that is evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that praiseth, is good: because if he praiseth, he doth also live well, because if he praiseth, not only with tongue he praiseth, but life also with tongue doth agree.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And there is the way whereby I will show him the salvation of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In sacrifice of praise “is the way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “the salvation of God”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ Jesus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how in sacrifice of praise to us is shown Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Christ with grace came to us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These words saith the Apostle: “But I live, now not I, but Christ liveth in me: but that in flesh I live, in faith I live of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave Himself for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Acknowledge then sinners, that there would not need physician, if they were whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Christ died for the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then they acknowledge their ungodlinesses, and first copy that Publican, saying, “Lord, be merciful to me a sinner:” show wounds, beseech Physician: and because they praise not themselves, but blame themselves,—“So that he that glorieth, not in himself but in the Lord may glory,”—they acknowledge the cause of the coming of Christ, because for this end He came, that He might save sinners: for “Jesus Christ came,” he saith, “into this world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Further, those Jews, boasting of their work, thus the same Apostle doth rebuke, in saying, that they to grace belonged not, who to their merits and their works thought that reward was owing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore that knoweth himself to belong to grace, doth know what is Christ and what is Christ’s because he needeth grace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If grace it is called, gratis it is given; if gratis it is given, not any merits of time have preceded that it should be given.…
